subheadline: Everything teams ask before building pages with the Desiderio content blocks.

items.question: Do I need to write code to use these blocks?
items.answer: No. Every block is a configurable content element in the TYPO3 page module. Editors fill in fields like headline, image, and button, and the block renders with the correct markup and styling. Developers only step in to add a brand new block type.

items.question: How do I change the look of every block at once?
items.answer: Switch the shadcn preset. Colours, radius, and typography come from semantic tokens, so re-skinning the theme restyles all blocks together. You never edit individual templates to change the palette, which keeps the design consistent.

items.question: Are the blocks accessible out of the box?
items.answer: Yes. Each block ships with semantic landmarks, a correct heading order, visible focus states, and alt-text fields. The library follows WCAG 2.2 AA, so you start from an accessible baseline instead of retrofitting it later.

items.question: Will the layouts work on mobile?
items.answer: Every block is mobile-first and responsive. Grids collapse to a single column, type scales down, and nothing overflows at narrow widths. You can preview each block at any screen size before publishing.

items.question: Can I translate the content?
items.answer: Yes. Blocks use standard TYPO3 localisation, so each language gets its own translated content while sharing one layout. Editor labels live in XLIFF files, which makes the backend translatable too.

items.question: What happens to my content if I switch themes?
items.answer: Your content is safe. Themes only change tokens and CSS, never the fields you filled in. Switching presets re-skins the page, but every headline, image, and link you entered stays exactly where it was.
